## Knowledge Retrieval

The digest above covers critical knowledge. For deeper exploration:
- `search_learnings` with keywords for your task
- `list_learnings` filtered by type or tags
- `get_learning <id>` for full content of any [short-id] referenced above

## Recording Learnings

When you discover something worth remembering, use `add_learning`.
Use `confidence: "high"` or tag with `digest` to ensure it surfaces in passive context.

### When to record

- Discover a non-obvious pattern or convention in this codebase
- Hit a gotcha or edge case that took time to debug
- Find important context that future sessions should know
- Complete an investigation and want to preserve the findings

### Learning types

| Type | When to use |
|------|-------------|
| `pattern` | Architectural decisions, code conventions |
| `gotcha` | Tricky issues, common mistakes, non-obvious behaviors |
| `investigation` | Debugging findings, root cause analyses |
| `documentation` | How things work, API behaviors |
| `tip` | Useful shortcuts, productivity hints |

## Project Structure

```
src/
├── index.ts           # MCP server entry point
├── cli.ts             # CLI entry point
├── types.ts           # Shared TypeScript types
├── tools/             # MCP tool implementations
├── db/                # SQLite database layer
│   ├── schema.ts      # Drizzle ORM schema
│   └── repository.ts  # Data access layer
├── digest/            # Digest generation for passive context
│   ├── types.ts       # DigestOptions, DigestResult types
│   ├── generator.ts   # Core scoring & filtering logic
│   └── formatter.ts   # Markdown, index, JSON output
├── embeddings/        # Vector embedding providers
├── config/            # Configuration management
├── api/               # REST API (optional)
└── cli/               # CLI commands
```

## Code Conventions

### TypeScript
- Strict mode enabled
- Use Zod for runtime validation (see `src/config/schema.ts`)
- Drizzle ORM for database operations

### Testing
- Vitest for testing
- Tests colocated: `*.test.ts` next to source files
- Run with `npm run test:run`

### Error Handling
- Tools should return descriptive error messages
- Use try/catch at tool boundaries

### Database
- SQLite with Drizzle ORM
- Schema in `src/db/schema.ts`
- All queries go through `src/db/repository.ts`

## Common Commands

```bash
npm run dev        # Development with hot reload
npm run build      # Build for production
npm run test:run   # Run tests once
npm run typecheck  # Type checking
npm run lint       # Linting
```

## Testing MCP Tools

```bash
npx @modelcontextprotocol/inspector node dist/index.js
```
